body:
Title: Canada Disability Benefit 2026 amount and SOR/2025-35 calculation rules.

Sources:
- https://laws-lois.justice.gc.ca/eng/regulations/SOR-2025-35/page-1.html
- https://www.canada.ca/en/services/benefits/disability/canada-disability-benefit/amount.html

Canada Disability Benefit Regulations, SOR/2025-35:
Section 2 eligibility criteria apply for months after May 2025 and include that the person is at least 18 years of age but not more than 65 years of age, is a DTC-eligible individual as defined in subsection 146.4(1) of the Income Tax Act, and is resident in Canada for purposes of the Income Tax Act with qualifying citizenship or immigration status.
Section 6 calculation of monthly benefit: the benefit amount for a payment period is the annual benefit amount, reduced by the reduction based on income, divided by 12.
For a beneficiary without a cohabiting spouse or common-law partner, the reduction based on income is the greater of zero and 20% x (C - D - (23000 x A)), where C is the beneficiary's adjusted income for the last taxation year before the payment period, D is the lesser of the beneficiary's working income and 10000 x A, and A is the indexing factor for the payment period.
For a beneficiary with a cohabiting spouse or common-law partner who is not a beneficiary, the reduction based on income is the greater of zero and 20% x (E - F - (32500 x A)), where E is adjusted income for the last taxation year before the payment period or the amount that would have been adjusted income with the current spouse or partner, F is the lesser of combined working income and 14000 x A, and A is the indexing factor.
For a beneficiary with a cohabiting spouse or common-law partner who is also a beneficiary, the reduction based on income is the greater of zero and 10% x (E - F - (32500 x A)), where E, F, and A have the same meanings for the two-beneficiary couple formula.
The indexing factor for a payment period is CPImax divided by CPI2024.

ESDC Canada Disability Benefit amount page:
Benefit amounts are based on adjusted family net income. Adjusted family net income is family net income from line 23600, plus the spouse or common-law partner's line 23600 if applicable, minus UCCB and RDSP income from lines 11700 and 12500, plus UCCB and RDSP repayments from lines 21300 and 23200.
For the period July 2026 to June 2027, the maximum monthly benefit is 204.20 and the benefit is based on adjusted family net income from the 2025 federal income tax return.
For the period July 2025 to June 2026, the maximum monthly benefit is 200 and the benefit is based on adjusted family net income from the 2024 federal income tax return.
For the period July 2026 to June 2027, if single, up to 10210 of working income is exempt when calculating the benefit. If the beneficiary has a spouse or common-law partner, up to 14294 of combined working income is exempt.
For the period July 2025 to June 2026, if single, up to 10000 of working income is exempt. If the beneficiary has a spouse or common-law partner, up to 14000 of combined working income is exempt.
Working income means income from employment, self-employment, and taxable scholarships.
For a single, separated, divorced, or widowed beneficiary, the calculation steps are to take adjusted family net income, subtract working income up to the exemption, subtract 23000, multiply the resulting positive amount by 0.20 or 20%, subtract that reduction from 2400, and divide by 12.
For a beneficiary with a spouse or common-law partner who is not eligible for the Canada Disability Benefit, the calculation steps are to take adjusted family net income, subtract combined working income up to the exemption, subtract 32500, multiply the resulting positive amount by 0.20 or 20%, subtract that reduction from 2400, and divide by 12.
For a beneficiary with a spouse or common-law partner who is also eligible for the Canada Disability Benefit, each person's calculation steps are to take adjusted family net income, subtract combined working income up to the exemption, subtract 32500, multiply the resulting positive amount by 0.10 or 10%, subtract that reduction from 2400, and divide by 12.
The July 2026 to June 2027 indexing factor implied by the published maximum monthly amount is 204.20 divided by 200, which equals 1.021; the same factor makes the indexed working income exemptions 10210 and 14294 from the base amounts 10000 and 14000.
